text_original: Motse said: What is the reason for the disorder in the world ?
text_normalized: Motse said: What is the reason for the disorder in the world ?

text_original: It is because the gentlemen of the world all under-stand trifles but not things of importance.
text_normalized: It is because the gentlemen of the world all under-stand trifles but not things of importance.

text_original: How do we know they understand trifles but not things of importance ?
text_normalized: How do we know they understand trifles but not things of importance ?

text_original: Because they do not understand the will of Heaven.
text_normalized: Because they do not understand the will of Heaven.

text_original: How do we know they do not understand the will of Heaven?
text_normalized: How do we know they do not understand the will of Heaven?

text_original: By observing the conduct in the family.
text_normalized: By observing the conduct in the family.

text_original: If a man commits a misdemeanour in the family he still has other families in which to seek shelter.
text_normalized: If a man commits a misdemeanour in the family he still has other families in which to seek shelter.

text_original: Yet, father reminds son, the elder brother reminds the younger brother, saying : Be obedient, be careful in conduct in the family.
text_normalized: Yet, father reminds son, the elder brother reminds the younger brother, saying : Be obedient, be careful in conduct in the family.

text_original: If one is not obedient and careful in conduct in the family how can he live in the state ?
text_normalized: If one is not obedient and careful in conduct in the family how can he live in the state ?

text_original: If a man commits a mis-demeanour in the state he still has other states in which to seek shelter.
text_normalized: If a man commits a mis-demeanour in the state he still has other states in which to seek shelter.

text_original: Yet father reminds son and the elder brother reminds the younger brother, saying : Be obedient.
text_normalized: Yet father reminds son and the elder brother reminds the younger brother, saying : Be obedient.

text_original: One cannot live in a state and be disobedient and careless.’' Now all men live in the world and under Heaven.
text_normalized: One cannot live in a state and be disobedient and careless.’' Now all men live in the world and under Heaven.

text_original: When a man sins against Heaven there is nowhere to seek shelter.
text_normalized: When a man sins against Heaven there is nowhere to seek shelter.

text_original: But people do not think of warning each other.
text_normalized: But people do not think of warning each other.

text_original: Thus I know that they do not understand things of importance.
text_normalized: Thus I know that they do not understand things of importance.

text_original: And Motse said: Be obedient.
text_normalized: And Motse said: Be obedient.

text_original: Be sure to do what Heaven desires and avoid what Heaven abominates.
text_normalized: Be sure to do what Heaven desires and avoid what Heaven abominates.

text_original: Now, what does Heaven desire and what does Heaven abominate ?
text_normalized: Now, what does Heaven desire and what does Heaven abominate ?

text_original: Heaven desires righteousness and abominates imrigliteousness.
text_normalized: Heaven desires righteousness and abominates imrigliteousness.

text_original: How do we know this ?
text_normalized: How do we know this ?

text_original: Because rigliteoixs-ness is tlie standard.
text_normalized: Because rigliteoixs-ness is tlie standard.

text_original: How do we know righteousness is the standard ?
text_normalized: How do we know righteousness is the standard ?

text_original: Because with righteousness the world will be orderly ; without it the world will he disorderly.
text_normalized: Because with righteousness the world will be orderly ; without it the world will he disorderly.

text_original: Soj I know righteousness is the standard.
text_normalized: Soj I know righteousness is the standard.

text_original: Now a standard is never given by the subordinate to the superior, it is always to be given by the superior to the subordinate.
text_normalized: Now a standard is never given by the subordinate to the superior, it is always to be given by the superior to the subordinate.

text_original: Hence the common people may not make the standard at will.
text_normalized: Hence the common people may not make the standard at will.

text_original: There are the scholars to give them the standard.
text_normalized: There are the scholars to give them the standard.

text_original: The scholars may not make the standard at will.
text_normalized: The scholars may not make the standard at will.

text_original: There are the ministers to give them the standard.
text_normalized: There are the ministers to give them the standard.

text_original: The ministers may not make the standard at will.
text_normalized: The ministers may not make the standard at will.

text_original: There are the feudal lords to give them the standard.
text_normalized: There are the feudal lords to give them the standard.

text_original: The feudal lords may not make the standard at will.
text_normalized: The feudal lords may not make the standard at will.

text_original: There is the high duke to give them the standard.
text_normalized: There is the high duke to give them the standard.

text_original: The high duke may not make the standard at will.
text_normalized: The high duke may not make the standard at will.

text_original: There is the emperor to give him the standard.
text_normalized: There is the emperor to give him the standard.

text_original: The emperor may not make the standard at will.
text_normalized: The emperor may not make the standard at will.

text_original: There is Heaven to give him the standard.
text_normalized: There is Heaven to give him the standard.

text_original: The gentlemen of the world all understand that the emperor gives the standard to the world but do not understand that Heaven gives the standard to the emperor.
text_normalized: The gentlemen of the world all understand that the emperor gives the standard to the world but do not understand that Heaven gives the standard to the emperor.

text_original: The sages, explaining this, said : ‘‘ When the emperor has done good, Heaven rewards him.
text_normalized: The sages, explaining this, said : ‘‘ When the emperor has done good, Heaven rewards him.

text_original: When the emperor has committed wrong, Heaven punishes him.
text_normalized: When the emperor has committed wrong, Heaven punishes him.

text_original: When the emperor is unjust in reward and punishment and not judicious in hearing lawsuits, Heaven visits him with disease and calamities, and frost and dew will be untimely,” The emperor will then have to feed oxen and sheep with grass and dogs and pigs with grains and prepare clean cakes and wine to pray and invoke Heaven for blessing.
text_normalized: When the emperor is unjust in reward and punishment and not judicious in hearing lawsuits, Heaven visits him with disease and calamities, and frost and dew will be untimely,” The emperor will then have to feed oxen and sheep with grass and dogs and pigs with grains and prepare clean cakes and wine to pray and invoke Heaven for blessing.

text_original: I have not yet heard of Heaven praying and invoking the emperor for WILL OF HEAVEN (III) blessing.
text_normalized: I have not yet heard of Heaven praying and invoking the emperor for WILL OF HEAVEN (III) blessing.

text_original: So, I can say Heaven is more honourable and wise than the emperor.
text_normalized: So, I can say Heaven is more honourable and wise than the emperor.

text_original: Therefore righteousness does not come from the ignorant and humble but from the honourable and wise.
text_normalized: Therefore righteousness does not come from the ignorant and humble but from the honourable and wise.

text_original: Who is honourable ?
text_normalized: Who is honourable ?

text_original: Heaven is honourable.
text_normalized: Heaven is honourable.

text_original: Who is wise ?
text_normalized: Who is wise ?

text_original: Heaven is wise.
text_normalized: Heaven is wise.

text_original: And, so, righteousness assuredly comes from Heaven.
text_normalized: And, so, righteousness assuredly comes from Heaven.

text_original: And the gentlemen of the world who desire to do righteousness therefore must obey the will of Heaven.
text_normalized: And the gentlemen of the world who desire to do righteousness therefore must obey the will of Heaven.

text_original: What is the will of Heaven that is to be obeyed ?
text_normalized: What is the will of Heaven that is to be obeyed ?

text_original: It is to love all the people in the world universally.
text_normalized: It is to love all the people in the world universally.

text_original: How do we know it is to love all the people in the world universally ?
text_normalized: How do we know it is to love all the people in the world universally ?

text_original: Because (Heaven) accepts sacrifices from all.
text_normalized: Because (Heaven) accepts sacrifices from all.

text_original: How do we know Heaven accepts sacrifices from all?
text_normalized: How do we know Heaven accepts sacrifices from all?

text_original: Because from antiquity to the present day there is no distant or isolated country but what feeds oxen and sheep, dogs and pigs with grass and grains, and prepare clean cakes and wine to worship God, hills and rivers, and the spirits.
text_normalized: Because from antiquity to the present day there is no distant or isolated country but what feeds oxen and sheep, dogs and pigs with grass and grains, and prepare clean cakes and wine to worship God, hills and rivers, and the spirits.

text_original: So we know Heaven accepts sacrifices from all Accepting sacrifice from all, Heaven must love them all.
text_normalized: So we know Heaven accepts sacrifices from all Accepting sacrifice from all, Heaven must love them all.

text_original: Take the lords of Ch’u and Yiieh for instance.
text_normalized: Take the lords of Ch’u and Yiieh for instance.

text_original: The lord of Ch'u accepts offering from all within the four borders of Ch’u, therefore he loves the people of Ch'u.
text_normalized: The lord of Ch'u accepts offering from all within the four borders of Ch’u, therefore he loves the people of Ch'u.

text_original: And, the lord of Yiieh accepts offering from all within the four borders of Yiieh, therefore he loves the people of Yiieh.
text_normalized: And, the lord of Yiieh accepts offering from all within the four borders of Yiieh, therefore he loves the people of Yiieh.

text_original: Now Heaven accepts offering from all the world and so I know Heaven loves all the people in the world.
text_normalized: Now Heaven accepts offering from all the world and so I know Heaven loves all the people in the world.

text_original: That Heaven loves all the people of the world is proved not only by this.
text_normalized: That Heaven loves all the people of the world is proved not only by this.

text_original: In all the countries in the world and among all the people who live on grains, the murder of one innocent individual brings down one calamity.
text_normalized: In all the countries in the world and among all the people who live on grains, the murder of one innocent individual brings down one calamity.

text_original: Now who is it that murders the ixmocent ?.
text_normalized: Now who is it that murders the ixmocent ?.

text_original: It is man.
text_normalized: It is man.

text_original: Who is it that sends down the calamity ?
text_normalized: Who is it that sends down the calamity ?

text_original: It is Heaven.
text_normalized: It is Heaven.

text_original: If Heaven really did not love the people, why.
text_normalized: If Heaven really did not love the people, why.

text_original: does Heaven send dowm calamities for the murder of the innocent ?
text_normalized: does Heaven send dowm calamities for the murder of the innocent ?

text_original: Furthermore, Heaven- loves the people dearly, Heaven loves the people inclusively.
text_normalized: Furthermore, Heaven- loves the people dearly, Heaven loves the people inclusively.

text_original: And this can be known.
text_normalized: And this can be known.

text_original: How do we know Heaven- loves the people ?
text_normalized: How do we know Heaven- loves the people ?

text_original: Because of the certain reward to the good and punishment to the evil from the virtuous (Heaven).
text_normalized: Because of the certain reward to the good and punishment to the evil from the virtuous (Heaven).

text_original: How do we know the virtuous (Heaven) certainly rewards the good and punishes the evil ?
text_normalized: How do we know the virtuous (Heaven) certainly rewards the good and punishes the evil ?

text_original: I know this from the (examples of) the sage-kings of the Three Dynasties.
text_normalized: I know this from the (examples of) the sage-kings of the Three Dynasties.

text_original: Anciently, the sage-kings of the Three Dynasties, Yao, Shun, Yti, T’ang, Wen, and Wu, loved the world universally and proceeded to benefit them.
text_normalized: Anciently, the sage-kings of the Three Dynasties, Yao, Shun, Yti, T’ang, Wen, and Wu, loved the world universally and proceeded to benefit them.

text_original: They converted the desires of the people and led them to worship God, hills and rivers, and the spirits.
text_normalized: They converted the desires of the people and led them to worship God, hills and rivers, and the spirits.

text_original: Heaven was pleased because they loved what it loved and benefited what it would benefit, and besto'wed rewards upon them, placing them on the throne, crowning them emperor, upholding them as the standard, and calling them sage-kings.
text_normalized: Heaven was pleased because they loved what it loved and benefited what it would benefit, and besto'wed rewards upon them, placing them on the throne, crowning them emperor, upholding them as the standard, and calling them sage-kings.

text_original: Here we have the proof of reward of the good.
text_normalized: Here we have the proof of reward of the good.

text_original: Anciently, the wicked kings of the Three Dynasties, Ohieh, Chow, Yu, and Li, hated all the world and proceeded to oppress them.
text_normalized: Anciently, the wicked kings of the Three Dynasties, Ohieh, Chow, Yu, and Li, hated all the world and proceeded to oppress them.

text_original: They converted the desires of the people and led them to blaspheme against God, hills and rivers, and the spirits.
text_normalized: They converted the desires of the people and led them to blaspheme against God, hills and rivers, and the spirits.

text_original: Heaven was offended because they hated those whom Heaven loved and oppressed those whom Heaven would benefit, and Heaven decreed punishments upon them, letting fathers and sons be scattered, their empire he put to an end, their state be lost to them, and capital punishment fall WILL OF HEAWBN (III) upon them.
text_normalized: Heaven was offended because they hated those whom Heaven loved and oppressed those whom Heaven would benefit, and Heaven decreed punishments upon them, letting fathers and sons be scattered, their empire he put to an end, their state be lost to them, and capital punishment fall WILL OF HEAWBN (III) upon them.

text_original: Thereupon, the multitudes in the world con-demned them, the condemnation lasting all through the ten thousand generations, the people calling them wicked kings.
text_normalized: Thereupon, the multitudes in the world con-demned them, the condemnation lasting all through the ten thousand generations, the people calling them wicked kings.

text_original: Here we have the proof of punishment of the evil.
text_normalized: Here we have the proof of punishment of the evil.

text_original: Those gentlemen of the world who desire to do righteous-ness have only to obey the will of Heaven.
text_normalized: Those gentlemen of the world who desire to do righteous-ness have only to obey the will of Heaven.

text_original: To obey the will of Heaven is to be universal and to oppose the will of Heaven is to be partial (in love).
text_normalized: To obey the will of Heaven is to be universal and to oppose the will of Heaven is to be partial (in love).

text_original: According to the doctrine of universality righteousness is the standard; in the doc-trine of partiality force is the basis of government.
text_normalized: According to the doctrine of universality righteousness is the standard; in the doc-trine of partiality force is the basis of government.

text_original: What is it like to have righteousness as the basis of government ?
text_normalized: What is it like to have righteousness as the basis of government ?

text_original: The great will not attack the small, the strong will not plunder the weak, the many will not oppress the few, the clever will not deceive the ignorant, the honoured will not disdain the humble, the rich will not mock the poor, and the young will not rob the old.
text_normalized: The great will not attack the small, the strong will not plunder the weak, the many will not oppress the few, the clever will not deceive the ignorant, the honoured will not disdain the humble, the rich will not mock the poor, and the young will not rob the old.

text_original: And the states in the empire will not ruin each other with water, fire, poison, and weapons.
text_normalized: And the states in the empire will not ruin each other with water, fire, poison, and weapons.

text_original: Such a regime will be benefi.cial to Heaven above, to the spirits in the middle sphere, to the people below.
text_normalized: Such a regime will be benefi.cial to Heaven above, to the spirits in the middle sphere, to the people below.

text_original: Being benefi.cial to these three it is benefi.cial to all.
text_normalized: Being benefi.cial to these three it is benefi.cial to all.

text_original: This is called the virtue of Heaven; whoever practises this is a sage, magnanimous, gracious, and righteous, loyal, afiectionate, and filial, and all such good names in the world will be gathered and atrributed to him.
text_normalized: This is called the virtue of Heaven; whoever practises this is a sage, magnanimous, gracious, and righteous, loyal, afiectionate, and filial, and all such good names in the world will be gathered and atrributed to him.

text_original: Because such conduct is in accordance with the will of Heaven.
text_normalized: Because such conduct is in accordance with the will of Heaven.

text_original: Now, what is it like to have force as the basis of govern-ment ?
text_normalized: Now, what is it like to have force as the basis of govern-ment ?

text_original: The great will attack the small, the strong will plunder the weak, the many will oppress the few, the clever will impose upon the ignorant, the honoured will disdain the humble, the rich will mock the poor, and the young will rob the old.
text_normalized: The great will attack the small, the strong will plunder the weak, the many will oppress the few, the clever will impose upon the ignorant, the honoured will disdain the humble, the rich will mock the poor, and the young will rob the old.

text_original: And the states in the empire will ruin each other with water, fire, poison, and weapons.
text_normalized: And the states in the empire will ruin each other with water, fire, poison, and weapons.

text_original: Such a regime will not be helpful to Heaven above, to the spirits in the middle sphere, or to the people below.
text_normalized: Such a regime will not be helpful to Heaven above, to the spirits in the middle sphere, or to the people below.

text_original: Hot being helpful to these three, it is not helpful to any.
text_normalized: Hot being helpful to these three, it is not helpful to any.

text_original: This is called the enemy of Heaven.
text_normalized: This is called the enemy of Heaven.

text_original: He who practises this is a bad man, not magnani-mous, not gracious, and unrighteous, disloyal, unaSectionate, and unfilial, and all such evil names in the world are gathered and attributed to him.
text_normalized: He who practises this is a bad man, not magnani-mous, not gracious, and unrighteous, disloyal, unaSectionate, and unfilial, and all such evil names in the world are gathered and attributed to him.

text_original: Because such conduct is in opposition to the will of Heaven.
text_normalized: Because such conduct is in opposition to the will of Heaven.

text_original: Hence Motse established the will of Heaven as his standard, just as the wheelwright uses his compasses and the carpenter uses his square as their standards.
text_normalized: Hence Motse established the will of Heaven as his standard, just as the wheelwright uses his compasses and the carpenter uses his square as their standards.

text_original: The wheelwright with his compasses and the carpenter with his square can judge the circularity and the squareness of objects.
text_normalized: The wheelwright with his compasses and the carpenter with his square can judge the circularity and the squareness of objects.

text_original: Similarly, with the will of Heaven as the standard, Motse can tell that the gentlemen of the world are far from righteousness.
text_normalized: Similarly, with the will of Heaven as the standard, Motse can tell that the gentlemen of the world are far from righteousness.

text_original: How do we know the gentlemen of the world are far from righteousness ?
text_normalized: How do we know the gentlemen of the world are far from righteousness ?

text_original: For, the lords in the large states compete in saying : ‘‘ Being a big state, if I do not attack the small states, in what way am I big ?
text_normalized: For, the lords in the large states compete in saying : ‘‘ Being a big state, if I do not attack the small states, in what way am I big ?

text_original: Therefore they mustered their warriors and soldiers, and arranged their boat and chariot forces to attack some innocent state.
text_normalized: Therefore they mustered their warriors and soldiers, and arranged their boat and chariot forces to attack some innocent state.

text_original: They broke into its borders, cut down its fields, felled its trees, tore down its inner and outer city walls, and filled up its moats and ditches, burned its ancestral temples and seized and Idiled its sacrificial victims.
text_normalized: They broke into its borders, cut down its fields, felled its trees, tore down its inner and outer city walls, and filled up its moats and ditches, burned its ancestral temples and seized and Idiled its sacrificial victims.

text_original: Of the people the strong were killed, the weak were brought back in chains and ropes.
text_normalized: Of the people the strong were killed, the weak were brought back in chains and ropes.

text_original: The men were turned into servants and grooms and prisoners.
text_normalized: The men were turned into servants and grooms and prisoners.

text_original: The WILL OF HEAVEN (III) women were made to be waitresses (to pour wine).
text_normalized: The WILL OF HEAVEN (III) women were made to be waitresses (to pour wine).

text_original: Yet, the warring lord did not even know that this is unmagnanimous and nnrigliteous.
text_normalized: Yet, the warring lord did not even know that this is unmagnanimous and nnrigliteous.

text_original: He announced to the neighbouring lords : I have attacked a state, defeated an army, and killed so many generals.’ And the neighbouring lords did not know that this is unmagnanimous and unrighteous either, but with furs and silk sent envoys to ofier congratulations.
text_normalized: He announced to the neighbouring lords : I have attacked a state, defeated an army, and killed so many generals.’ And the neighbouring lords did not know that this is unmagnanimous and unrighteous either, but with furs and silk sent envoys to ofier congratulations.

text_original: And the warring lords were even doubly ignorant of its being immagnanimous and unrighteous.
text_normalized: And the warring lords were even doubly ignorant of its being immagnanimous and unrighteous.

text_original: They recorded it on the bamboos and silk and kept them in the archives so that the descendants would imitate their royal ancestors, saying: Why not let us open up the archives and let us learn of the achievements of our ancestors ?
text_normalized: They recorded it on the bamboos and silk and kept them in the archives so that the descendants would imitate their royal ancestors, saying: Why not let us open up the archives and let us learn of the achievements of our ancestors ?

text_original: ” Then they would surely not learn : Such and such is the regime of Wu,” but would learn : I have attacked states, reversed armies, and killed so many of their generals.’’ Now that the warring lords do not understand this to be unmagnanimous and unrighteous, and neighbouring lords do not understand this to be unmagnanimous and unrighteous, therefore attacks and assaults go on generation after generation without end.
text_normalized: ” Then they would surely not learn : Such and such is the regime of Wu,” but would learn : I have attacked states, reversed armies, and killed so many of their generals.’’ Now that the warring lords do not understand this to be unmagnanimous and unrighteous, and neighbouring lords do not understand this to be unmagnanimous and unrighteous, therefore attacks and assaults go on generation after generation without end.

text_original: What do I mean when I say people do not understand things of importance but understand trifles ?
text_normalized: What do I mean when I say people do not understand things of importance but understand trifles ?

text_original: Supposing some one entered the orchard and garden of another and took the other’s peaches and prunes, melon and ginger, he will be punished by the superior when caught and condemned by the public when heard of.
text_normalized: Supposing some one entered the orchard and garden of another and took the other’s peaches and prunes, melon and ginger, he will be punished by the superior when caught and condemned by the public when heard of.

text_original: Because he did not share the toil hut takes the fruit and appropriates what is not his.
text_normalized: Because he did not share the toil hut takes the fruit and appropriates what is not his.

text_original: How much more is this true with him who jumps over another’s fence and maltreats the children of the other ; of hir)(> who digs into another’s storehouse and carries away the other’s gold, jade, silk, and cloth ; of him who breaks into another’s fold and steals the other's oxen and horses ; and of him who kills an innocent person ?
text_normalized: How much more is this true with him who jumps over another’s fence and maltreats the children of the other ; of hir)(> who digs into another’s storehouse and carries away the other’s gold, jade, silk, and cloth ; of him who breaks into another’s fold and steals the other's oxen and horses ; and of him who kills an innocent person ?

text_original: In the govermnent of the lords of to-day all— from the one who kills an innocent person to the one who jumps over another’s fence and maltreats the other’s children, who digs into another’s warehouse and carries away his gold, jade, silk and cloth, who breaks into another’s fold and steals his oxen and horses, and who enters another’s orchard and garden and takes Ms peaches and prunes, melons and ginger — all these are punished quite the same as they would be even in the government of Yao, Shun, Yli, T’ang, Wen, and Wu.
text_normalized: In the govermnent of the lords of to-day all— from the one who kills an innocent person to the one who jumps over another’s fence and maltreats the other’s children, who digs into another’s warehouse and carries away his gold, jade, silk and cloth, who breaks into another’s fold and steals his oxen and horses, and who enters another’s orchard and garden and takes Ms peaches and prunes, melons and ginger — all these are punished quite the same as they would be even in the government of Yao, Shun, Yli, T’ang, Wen, and Wu.

text_original: Now the lords and chiefs in the world all attack and absorb others.
text_normalized: Now the lords and chiefs in the world all attack and absorb others.

text_original: This is a thousand and ten thousand times worse than killing one innocent individual, a thousand and ten thousand times worse than jumping over another’s fence and maltreating his children or digging into another’s storehouse and carrying away his gold, jade, silk, and cloth, a thousand and ten thousand times worse than breaking into another’s fold and stealing his oxen and horses, or entering another’s orchard and garden and taking his peaches and prunes, melons and ginger.
text_normalized: This is a thousand and ten thousand times worse than killing one innocent individual, a thousand and ten thousand times worse than jumping over another’s fence and maltreating his children or digging into another’s storehouse and carrying away his gold, jade, silk, and cloth, a thousand and ten thousand times worse than breaking into another’s fold and stealing his oxen and horses, or entering another’s orchard and garden and taking his peaches and prunes, melons and ginger.

text_original: Yet, they claim it to be righteous.
text_normalized: Yet, they claim it to be righteous.

text_original: Motse said : This is meant to confuse us.
text_normalized: Motse said : This is meant to confuse us.

text_original: And is this at all different from confusion in the distinctions between black and white, and sweet and bitter ?
text_normalized: And is this at all different from confusion in the distinctions between black and white, and sweet and bitter ?

text_original: Suppose a man who upon being shown a little blackness says it is black, but upon being shown much blackness says it is white.
text_normalized: Suppose a man who upon being shown a little blackness says it is black, but upon being shown much blackness says it is white.

text_original: He will have to admit that his sight is confused and that he cannot tell the difference between black and white.
text_normalized: He will have to admit that his sight is confused and that he cannot tell the difference between black and white.

text_original: Suppose a man when served with a little bitter says it is bitter, but WILL OF HEAVEN (III) when served witli mucli bitter says it is sweet.
text_normalized: Suppose a man when served with a little bitter says it is bitter, but WILL OF HEAVEN (III) when served witli mucli bitter says it is sweet.

text_original: Tlien lie will Lave to admit that bis taste is impaired and that he cannot tell the difference between sweet and bitter.
text_normalized: Tlien lie will Lave to admit that bis taste is impaired and that he cannot tell the difference between sweet and bitter.

text_original: In the government of the present lords, the murderer of an individual is imprisoned by the state.
text_normalized: In the government of the present lords, the murderer of an individual is imprisoned by the state.

text_original: But the murderer of many men of the neighbouring states is upheld as righteous.
text_normalized: But the murderer of many men of the neighbouring states is upheld as righteous.

text_original: How is this different from confusing the distinction between black and white and sweet and bitter ?
text_normalized: How is this different from confusing the distinction between black and white and sweet and bitter ?

text_original: Therefore Motse established the will of Heaven to be the standard.
text_normalized: Therefore Motse established the will of Heaven to be the standard.

text_original: Not only Motse established the will of Heaven to be the standard, it is also the theme of an ode in the Ta Ya” among the books of the ancient kings: ‘'God said to King Wen, ‘I cherish your intelligent virtue.
text_normalized: Not only Motse established the will of Heaven to be the standard, it is also the theme of an ode in the Ta Ya” among the books of the ancient kings: ‘'God said to King Wen, ‘I cherish your intelligent virtue.

text_original: It was not proclaimed with much noise or gesture.
text_normalized: It was not proclaimed with much noise or gesture.

text_original: It was not modified after the possession of the empire.
text_normalized: It was not modified after the possession of the empire.

text_original: Instinctively and naturally submissive to the scheme of God.’ ”  This is to proclaim that King Wen used the will of Heaven as standard and was submissive to God’s scheme.
text_normalized: Instinctively and naturally submissive to the scheme of God.’ ”  This is to proclaim that King Wen used the will of Heaven as standard and was submissive to God’s scheme.

text_original: If the gentle-men of the world really desire to practise magnanimity and righteousness and be superior men, seeking to attain the way of the sage-kings on the one hand and to procure blessings to the people on the other, they must not neglect to under-stand the will of Heaven.
text_normalized: If the gentle-men of the world really desire to practise magnanimity and righteousness and be superior men, seeking to attain the way of the sage-kings on the one hand and to procure blessings to the people on the other, they must not neglect to under-stand the will of Heaven.

text_original: The will of Heaven is truly the standard of righteousness.
text_normalized: The will of Heaven is truly the standard of righteousness.
